what_it_does: Documents the end-to-end mechanism for how a decision card's 'Ask about this' explanation is triggered, how explanatory context/grounding is resolved, and how the decision address is resolved, mapping three serial joints and their current breakages.
observations: Commissioned by Tim on 2026-06-21 and investigated by three parallel read-only agents (trigger, context-resolution, address-resolution), synthesized by projection.
Frames the flow as three serial joints: trigger, context resolution, and address resolution, all of which must connect for a genuinely grounded walk-through.

The card's 'Ask about this', V/RHM ask, and per-unit brain all route to the generic POST /api/claude/turn via a single constant; the grounded POST /api/decision/explain endpoint has zero client-side references.
A trigger redirect fix has been built and shape-verified but is being held until Part 2 coverage is complete.
On the default fast path, explanation_grounding includes only the card's own authored fields: meaning, legibility.is, options with implications, and legibility.why.

Two provenance channels exist but gate to empty on the fast path: WHERE THIS CAME FROM requires a non-empty explanation_source, and RELATED BACKGROUND requires rerank_score >= 0.5 but rerank is off by default.
The decision record's DECISION_FIELDS include id, address, meaning, options, explanation_source, scope, legibility, subtype, owner; explanation_source is the only provenance-bearing field.
resolve_address returns the declared row and composed state but does not dereference explanation_source or reach corpus.
Only about 8-9 of roughly 21-24 decisions carry explanation_source; the rest lack any provenance link.
The serial gate chain requires: door wired, pointer present, and pointer has real content.
Corroborates recollection's prior diagnosis (8e3d1f1) and adds the trigger-unwired finding plus the rerank fast-path tradeoff.
Ownership is split across projection (trigger), recollection/composition (grounding mechanism + backfill), and upstream (provenance capture).
standouts: The grounded explain endpoint is completely unwired client-side, so even cards that could ground correctly are explained by the generic streamer.
The fast path intentionally reduces grounding to the card's own text because explanation_source is usually absent and corpus rerank is disabled for latency.
explanation_source is the sole provenance-bearing field and is missing for the majority of decisions, making upstream capture the binding constraint.
conventions: Annotates claims with file:line references and labels evidence as Observed unless marked Inferred.
Uses a circuit diagram and a serial gate chain to structure diagnosis.
Assigns each part to an owner/lane (projection, recollection, composition, upstream).
Frames failure modes as 'default-to-wrong' and defensive gating.
Distinguishes fast-path behavior from deep/slow-path behavior.
concerns: A built fix is intentionally held, creating a pending integration state that could drift or be forgotten.
Genuine grounding depends almost entirely on a single optional field (explanation_source), making coverage fragile.
The fast path deliberately disables corpus retrieval, so latency is traded for depth by design.
Multiple ownership boundaries mean the L1 close requires coordinated handoffs across trigger, backfill, and upstream capture.
notes: A code-grounded mechanism map that says the grounded explanation is broken at three different serial joints, with the trigger fix already built but held pending provenance coverage.
questions: What exact shape-verification was performed on the poised trigger redirect?
What determines which ~12 decisions receive explanation_source backfill and in what order?
What is the user-visible L1 symptom or issue this fix is meant to resolve?
What rerank latency threshold or condition would make enabling corpus admission on the fast path acceptable?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is explicitly a grounded walk-through / mechanism map that diagnoses how the explain feature is set off and how it works.
apparent_themes: Serial dependency: every joint must connect for a grounded explanation.
Provenance must be captured upstream before it can be retrieved.
Latency versus depth tradeoff in interactive explanation.
Defensive gating as a response to earlier wrong-decision bleed.
Multi-agent investigation synthesized into a single actionable map.
Default-to-wrong diagnosis rather than assuming retrieval is broken.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the grounded explanation pipeline inspectable and to coordinate a sequenced, three-part fix across trigger wiring, provenance backfill, and upstream capture so that card explanations cite real origin content.
novelty: It treats explanation as a serial circuit with three distinct joints (trigger, context, address) and diagnoses each as broken in a different way, rather than treating grounding as a single retrieval bug.
contribution: Provides a code-grounded, multi-joint diagnosis of the grounded explanation pipeline and a clear ownership map for the pending fix, which a fused system would keep as the reference mechanism map.
summary_for_embedding: A mechanism-map document tracing the trigger, context-resolution, and address-resolution joints of a decision-card grounded explanation feature, noting that the grounded endpoint is unwired, the fast path only uses card-authored fields, and provenance depends on a sparse explanation_source field.
intention_for_embedding: To record how the grounded explanation is supposed to fire and why it currently restates card text, so the L1 fix can be executed in the right order across trigger, backfill, and upstream capture.
